subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the chemical properties of acids and bases with balanced equations
Understand the concept of H+ and OH- ion concentration in solutions
Correlate the strength of acids and bases with the pH scale
Analyze the importance of pH in biological and environmental systems
Understand the manufacturing process and chemical reactions of industrially important salts
Investigate the nature of 'Water of Crystallization' through experimentation

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Reactions of Acids and Bases
topics_covered: Reaction with metals and formation of Hydrogen gas
Reaction with Metal Carbonates and Hydrogen Carbonates
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of the 'Pop' test with Zinc and $HCl$
Step-by-step derivation of balanced chemical equations
Comparison of reactions with Metallic vs. Non-metallic oxides
student_activities: Write balanced equations for the reaction of $HCl$ and $CH_{3}COOH$ with Magnesium
Observation table for $CO_{2}$ evolution with Lime Water
assessment: Oral quiz on products formed during neutralization

lesson_plan.title: Commonality in Acids and Bases
topics_covered: Role of water in dissociation (H+ and OH- ions)
Difference between Strong and Weak acids/bases
teaching_strategy: Circuit diagram demonstration showing bulb glowing in $HCl$ vs. Glucose
Explaining why dry $HCl$ gas does not change litmus color
Dilution process and its exothermic nature
student_activities: Draw the experimental setup for testing conductivity
Group discussion: Why are alcohols not classified as acids?
assessment: Notebook review of the dilution safety warning

lesson_plan.title: Strength of Acids and Bases (pH)
topics_covered: Universal Indicator and the pH scale
teaching_strategy: Color-matching activity with Universal Indicator
Using pH paper on different concentrations of $HCl$ and Sodium Hydroxide
Comparative chart of $H^{+}$ ion concentration
student_activities: Test the pH of common substances (Saliva, Lemon juice, Coffee)
Plot a bar graph of pH values for 5 different solutions
assessment: MCQs on pH values and ion concentration
Exit slip: Describe the difference between a weak acid and a dilute acid

lesson_plan.title: Importance of pH in Daily Life
topics_covered: pH of the digestive system and Antacids
Tooth decay and the role of Calcium Hydroxyapatite
teaching_strategy: Case studies on acid rain and aquatic life
Discussion on why Methanoic acid causes pain in stings
Analyzing the buffering action of soil
student_activities: Research activity: Finding the pH range of a healthy garden soil

lesson_plan.title: The Family of Salts - Part 1
topics_covered: pH of salts (Acidic, Basic, and Neutral salts)
teaching_strategy: Flowchart for the electrolysis of Brine
Using litmus to test salts like $NH_{4}Cl$ and $CH_{3}COONa$
Displaying industrial uses of Chlorine and Hydrogen
student_activities: Predict the nature of salts based on parent acid and base
assessment: Matching activity: Product to Industrial use

lesson_plan.title: The Family of Salts - Part 2
topics_covered: The concept of Water of Crystallization
teaching_strategy: Chemical equations for heating Baking Soda
Demonstrating the reversible dehydration of Copper Sulphate
Explaining the fractional water molecule in POP
student_activities: Observe the change in blue $CuSO_{4}$ crystals upon heating
Write the equation for the setting of Plaster of Paris
assessment: Worksheet on uses of Sodium compounds

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Comprehensive Assessment
teaching_strategy: Mind-map construction (Acids $ightarrow$ Bases $ightarrow$ Salts)
Solving NCERT exercise 'Back Questions'
Clarifying doubts on $H_{3}O^{+}$ formation
student_activities: Participate in a group 'Reaction Balancing' relay

teaching_aids: Universal Indicator, pH strips, conductivity kit
Samples: Copper Sulphate, Gypsum, Baking Soda, Bleaching Powder
evaluation_methods: Identification of 'X' and 'Y' in chemical reactions
teacher_tip: Students often struggle to understand why $H^{+}$ is written as $H_{3}O^{+}$ (Hydronium). Use a visual analogy of a 'homeless' Hydrogen ion finding a 'home' with a Water molecule to make it more memorable.
